seoTitle: How to Save Email Attachments to SharePoint With Power Automate
metaDescription: Learn how to automatically save Outlook email attachments to a SharePoint document library using Power Automate.
intro: Manually saving email attachments to SharePoint is tedious and easy to forget. This guide shows how to build a Power Automate flow that watches for new emails and saves their attachments straight into a SharePoint document library.

steps.heading: Create an automated cloud flow
steps.body: Start a new flow in Power Automate using an automated trigger tied to your Outlook mailbox.

steps.heading: Configure the Outlook email trigger
steps.body: Set the trigger to fire when a new email arrives, and narrow it down by sender, subject, or folder as needed.

steps.heading: Extract email attachments
steps.body: Add steps to pull the attachments out of the incoming email so they can be processed individually.

steps.heading: Save files to SharePoint
steps.body: Use a SharePoint action to create the file in your chosen document library, organizing it into folders if needed.

steps.heading: Avoid duplicates and organize files
steps.body: Add logic to check for existing files or naming conflicts so the same attachment isn't saved multiple times.

faq.question: Can I filter which emails trigger the flow?
faq.answer: Yes, you can filter by sender, subject line, or folder so only relevant emails trigger the attachment save.

faq.question: What happens if an email has multiple attachments?
faq.answer: The flow can loop through each attachment individually and save all of them to SharePoint.

faq.question: How do I avoid saving duplicate files?
faq.answer: You can add a check within the flow to see if a file with the same name already exists before saving a new copy.

faq.question: Can attachments be organized into different folders automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use conditions based on sender or subject to route attachments into different SharePoint folders.
